contentBlocks.original:
श्रुति स्मृति पुराणानाम् आलयम करुणालयम्।
नमामि भगवत्पादम शंकरं लोक शंकरम्॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śruti smṛti purāṇānām ālayama karuṇālayam.
namāmi bhagavatpādama śaṅkaraṃ loka śaṅkaram..
contentBlocks.translation: I bow at the feet of the Lord in the form Sri Sankaracharya, who is the blessing for humanity, who is the shrine for the sruti, the smrti and the purana, and, who is the abode of compassion.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रुति (śruti) – Vedas; स्मृति (smṛti) – tradition; पुराणानाम् (purāṇānām) – of the Puranas; आलयम् (ālayam) – abode; करुणालयम् (karuṇālayam) – abode of compassion (karuṇa:compassion | ālayam:abode); नमामि (namāmi) – I bow; भगवत्पादम् (bhagavatpādam) – at the feet of the Lord (bhagavat:of the Lord | pādam:feet); शंकरं (śaṅkaram) – Shankara; लोक (loka) – world; शंकरम् (śaṅkaram) – benefactor;
